Representative_Docs: ["The actual people in the classrooms. Who are running the school. To start making the decisions about how the school is run. [SOL-TEACHER-LEVEL> [PART-VOICE> 65 - 65 (1) [TrB_CBQL3_NN]: To improve the situation. They are not telling the people who work in the classrooms. Which is counterproductive in the extreme. We're going to start to measure some growth and some outcomes, and then we come back to the whole staff and we share, hey, everybody, this is what we did. [PART-VOICE> [GOV-ACCOUNTABILITY> 190 - 190 (1) [TrB_CBQL3_NN]: And that's a deliberate strategy to engage the team and to feel like they are a committee. And they're also very talented at what they do. I will sit here. [PART-VOICE> [COLLAB-TEAM> 205 - 205 (1) [TrB_CBQL3_NN]: One of the greatest shocks to me as a leader in education is to see teachers being informed of decisions that have been made that directly affect them without any consultation. And it seems to be the way that [PART-VOICE> [PART-INITIATIVE> 216 - 216 (1) [TrB_CBQL3_NN]: ...being left out of discussions affecting them, comes across very badly in an international school. It might be normal in Vietnam, but in an international school, it comes across very badly. And people, because the possibility is that a wrong decision is being made, that point hasn't been raised, and also that the people who are going to run it have not been involved in the process of developing it. [PART-VOICE> [PART-SUPPORTED> [PART-INITIATIVE> 217 - 217 (1) [TrB_CBQL3_NN]: And therefore, what we would call, as a school leader, if these guys don't know what this is, but it's buy-in. Buy-in, where you actually involve the people who are leading it to think it's a good idea, so that it's a success. But a lot of the decisions, for example, the course guides. [PART-VOICE> [PART-INITIATIVE> 218 - 218 (1) [TrB_CBQL3_NN]: Now the administration in the school, sense of loyalty and commitment in that area seems to be very strong, but unfortunately they're not experts in running schools and that creates a problem. So they're not actually in the classroom, they're not teaching. So it's lovely to have an office that is very committed, but you need the people in the classrooms committed. [SOL-TEACHER-LEVEL> [PART-VOICE> 244 - 244 (1) [TrB_CBQL3_NN]: I think the openness for the teachers to be able to make their decisions of how they want their school to run and have a voice in that is the key for satisfaction. [QA-DATA-USE> [SOL-CONSTRUCTIVE> [CHAL-QA-PROC> [CHAL-POLICY-PRACTICE> 124 - 124 (1) [TrB_CBQL3_NN]: Yes. So difficulties in leadership. I've spoken a lot about them. And then start to try and help. When that help is not utilized, it's very difficult to stay in that environment and watch the same challenges continue.
